text: Agile methodology is a way of managing projects, especially in software development. It focuses on breaking work into small cycles called sprints, which usually last two to four weeks. The team begins by creating a product backlog, a list of tasks or features to be completed. These are described in simple language as user stories, explaining what a user needs. Every morning, the team holds a short standup meeting to discuss progress and problems. Instead of finishing all work at once, Agile uses iterative development, meaning the team improves the product in small steps. This allows them to respond quickly to changes and feedback.
